production_note: agentic_K1 dual-LLM skeptic: sealed receipt write; packet-only; no bulk writers; no identity stamps
knowledge_boundary: Only landing + prior + active packet content.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
cli=pd.DataFrame({'cliente_id':['C001','C002']})
tx=pd.DataFrame({'cliente_id':['C001'],'monto':[1.0]})
print(len(cli.merge(tx, on='cliente_id', how='left')))
exercises.justification_from_packet: Left merge clientes–tx conserva C002 aunque no tenga transacciones; len del resultado es 2 (cardinalidad left, T1-A).

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
cli = pd.DataFrame({"cliente_id": ["C001", "C001"]})
print(bool(cli["cliente_id"].is_unique))
exercises.justification_from_packet: Unicidad de clave 1:1: cli['cliente_id'].is_unique es False si C001 está duplicado en el fixture (T1-A).

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
cli = pd.DataFrame({"cliente_id": ["C001"]})
tx = pd.DataFrame({"cliente_id": ["C001"] * 3, "monto": [1.0, 2.0, 3.0]})
print(len(cli.merge(tx, on="cliente_id", how="inner")))
exercises.justification_from_packet: Fan-out 1:m: un cliente con 3 tx produce 3 filas en inner merge; hay que documentar la multiplicación (T1-A).

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
cli = pd.DataFrame({"cliente_id": ["C001", "C002"]})
tx = pd.DataFrame({"cliente_id": ["C001"], "monto": [1.0]})
m = cli.merge(tx, on="cliente_id", how="left", indicator=True)
print(m.loc[m["_merge"] == "left_only", "cliente_id"].tolist())
exercises.justification_from_packet: Anti-join con indicator=True: _merge=='left_only' lista huérfanos; en el fixture → ['C002'] (T1-B).

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
a = pd.DataFrame({"id": [1]})
b = pd.DataFrame({"id": [1, 1]})
try:
    a.merge(b, on="id", validate="one_to_one")
except Exception:
    print("fail")
exercises.justification_from_packet: validate='one_to_one' hace fallar el merge si hay dups de clave; ante MergeError se imprime 'fail' (gate T1-B).

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
cli = pd.DataFrame({"cliente_id": ["C001", "C002", "C003"]})
tx = pd.DataFrame({"cliente_id": ["C001"]})
m = cli.merge(tx, on="cliente_id", how="left", indicator=True)
print(int((m["_merge"] == "left_only").sum()))
exercises.justification_from_packet: Conteo de huérfanos left_only tras left merge: 2 clientes sin match en tx del fixture (T1-B).

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({"id": [1, 2], "a": [10, 20], "b": [3, 4]})
print(len(df.melt(id_vars="id", value_vars=["a", "b"])))
exercises.justification_from_packet: melt wide→long: 2 filas × columnas a,b producen 4 filas long; es el reshape del theory T2-A.

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
long = pd.DataFrame({"id": [1, 1], "k": ["a", "b"], "v": [1.0, 2.0]})
w = long.pivot_table(index="id", columns="k", values="v", aggfunc="sum").reset_index()
print(w.columns.tolist())
exercises.justification_from_packet: pivot_table long→wide con aggfunc sum + reset_index deja columnas ['id','a','b'] estables (T2-A).

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
a = pd.DataFrame({"x": [1]})
b = pd.DataFrame({"x": [2]})
print(len(pd.concat([a, b], ignore_index=True)))
exercises.justification_from_packet: concat vertical con ignore_index apila dos DF de una fila → len 2; sin reindex forzado incorrecto (T2-A).

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
long = pd.DataFrame({"id": [1, 1], "mes": ["e", "f"], "monto": [1.0, 2.0]})
w = long.pivot(index="id", columns="mes", values="monto")
w = w.rename(columns=lambda c: f"monto_{c}")
print(list(w.columns))
exercises.justification_from_packet: Tras pivot, renombrar a monto_e/monto_f da nombres estables del portfolio; evita MultiIndex crudo (T2-B).

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame(columns=["cliente_id", "monto_ene"])
expected = {"cliente_id", "monto_ene"}
print(set(df.columns) == expected)
exercises.justification_from_packet: Validación de schema: set(columns)==expected → True cuando cliente_id y monto_ene están presentes (T2-B).

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({"a": [1]})
print(df.rename(columns={"a": "monto"}).columns.tolist())
exercises.justification_from_packet: rename(columns={'a':'monto'}) es explícito y auditable; columns resultantes ['monto'] (T2-B).

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({"region": ["Lima", "Lima", "Cusco"], "monto": [1.0, 2.0, 3.0]})
print(df.groupby("region")["monto"].sum().to_dict())
exercises.justification_from_packet: groupby+sum colapsa por región: en el fixture Cusco 3.0 y Lima 3.0; una fila por clave de grupo (T3-A).

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({"region": ["Lima", "Lima", "Cusco"], "monto": [1.0, 3.0, 2.0]})
print(df.groupby("region")["monto"].transform("mean").tolist())
exercises.justification_from_packet: transform('mean') reinyecta el agregado al shape original: lista [2.0,2.0,2.0] alineada a filas (T3-A).

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({"region": ["Lima", "Cusco"], "monto": [1.0, 2.0]})
agg = df.groupby("region", as_index=False).agg(total=("monto", "sum"), n=("monto", "count"))
print(agg.columns.tolist())
exercises.justification_from_packet: named agg con as_index=False produce columnas ['region','total','n']; nombres legibles para el resumen ejecutivo (T3-A).

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
s = pd.Series([1.0, 2.0, 3.0]).rolling(2).mean()
print([None if pd.isna(v) else float(v) for v in s])
exercises.justification_from_packet: rolling(2).mean sobre [1,2,3] deja NaN inicial como None y luego 1.5, 2.5 — ventana móvil documentada (T3-B).

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
df = pd.DataFrame({
    "cliente_id": ["C001", "C001", "C002"],
    "fecha": pd.to_datetime(["2024-01-05", "2024-03-01", "2024-02-10"]),
})
first = df.groupby("cliente_id")["fecha"].min()
cohort = first.dt.strftime("%Y-%m")
print(cohort.to_dict())
exercises.justification_from_packet: Cohorte = min fecha por id formateada YYYY-MM; asigna el mes de la primera observación del cliente (T3-B).

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
s = pd.Series([3.0, 1.0, 2.0], index=pd.to_datetime(["2024-01-03", "2024-01-01", "2024-01-02"]))
out = s.sort_index().rolling(2).mean()
print(float(out.iloc[-1]))
exercises.justification_from_packet: Orden temporal antes de rolling; último mean=2.0.

exercises.code:
parts = [10.0, 20.0, 70.0]
total = 100.0
print(abs(sum(parts) - total) < 1e-9)
exercises.justification_from_packet: Reconciliación con eps: abs(sum(parts)-total)<1e-9 evita igualdad float estricta; parts 10+20+70 vs 100 → True (T4-A).

exercises.code:
activos = 40
pagados = 10
print(pagados / activos)
exercises.justification_from_packet: Tasa con denominador correcto: pagados/activos = 10/40 = 0.25; no usar 25% ni pagados como base (T4-A).

exercises.code:
total = 100.0
lima = 60.0
print(total - lima)
exercises.justification_from_packet: Bridge residual del portfolio: total - lima = 100-60 = 40.0; primer escalón de la bridge table (T4-A).

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
tx = pd.DataFrame({"fecha": pd.to_datetime(["2024-01-01", "2024-02-01"]), "monto": [1.0, 9.0]})
cutoff = pd.Timestamp("2024-01-31")
print(tx.loc[tx["fecha"] <= cutoff, "monto"].tolist())
exercises.justification_from_packet: Filtro pre-cutoff: fechas <= 2024-01-31 dejan monto [1.0]; excluye el post-cutoff 9.0 (T4-B).

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
tx = pd.DataFrame({"fecha": pd.to_datetime(["2024-01-01", "2024-03-01"]), "monto": [10.0, 5.0]})
cutoff = pd.Timestamp("2024-01-31")
pre = tx.loc[tx["fecha"] <= cutoff, "monto"].sum()
print(float(tx["monto"].sum() - pre))
exercises.justification_from_packet: Delta de leakage = sum_total - sum_pre: el 5.0 post-cutoff no debe entrar en features del pasado (T4-B).

exercises.code:
import pandas as pd
tx = pd.DataFrame({
    "cliente_id": ["C001", "C001"],
    "fecha": pd.to_datetime(["2024-01-01", "2024-05-01"]),
    "monto": [3.0, 10.0],
})
cutoff = pd.Timestamp("2024-02-01")
pre = tx[tx["fecha"] <= cutoff]
print(pre.groupby("cliente_id")["monto"].max().to_dict())
exercises.justification_from_packet: Feature as-of max monto ≤cutoff → {'C001': 3.0}.

selfcheck.justification_from_packet: validate one_to_one falla si la cardinalidad no es 1:1.
left_only = filas del left sin match en right (anti-join).
transform reinyecta el agregado al shape original.
Leakage: datos posteriores al cutoff en features/métricas del pasado.
Si suma partes ≠ total: documentar diff con eps o corregir el corte.
